_manifest.description: Source-of-truth manifest of GitHub repository branch rulesets for Odisena Console. GitHub's REST API accepts ONE payload per ruleset (POST /repos/{owner}/{repo}/rulesets), so this file is a manifest: each element of `rulesets[]` is a complete, API-valid payload that the adjacent apply-rulesets.sh iterates and POSTs individually. Keys prefixed with `_` are non-enforced documentation/metadata and are stripped before any API call.

review_gradient.main: 2 approving reviews (production bar) + required signatures + linear history + squash-only
review_gradient.preview: 1 approving review, linear history, no signature requirement
review_gradient.develop: 0 approving reviews (PR still required for tracking), merge commits allowed

RFPA.R_recover: Every promotion must be able to recover the prior known-good state (reversible, non-destructive deploy posture).
RFPA.F_field: Changes are exercised in a real field/environment (develop -> preview -> main) before promotion.
RFPA.P_principal: A single accountable principal owns each promotion decision (solo-operator today; scales to CODEOWNERS).
RFPA.A_assumption: Assumptions behind a promotion are written down in the A/V/P/T log and re-checked each cycle.

AVPT.attempt: One deployment mutation per Attempt (single reversible change per cycle).
AVPT.validate: Validate via CI and/or environment evidence (preview deploy health, checks, manual smoke of the PWA per DEPLOYMENT.md 'Verifying a deployment').
AVPT.preserve: Preserve the highest known-good deploy/rollback state (record the last-good commit/deploy id; keep it re-deployable).
AVPT.track: Track the delta and evidence before the next cycle.

gates: At least one AVPT cycle MUST be completed and logged before any merge/promotion.
No cycle N+1 promotion until cycle N has a written A/V/P/T log.
Preserve a reversible / non-destructive deployment posture at all times (static host: keep previous deploy re-promotable / instant rollback enabled).
Promotion order is develop -> preview -> main; main promotion requires a preview A/V/P/T log referencing the candidate commit.
release_governance.avpt_log_location_proposed: docs/releases/AVPT-<cycle>-<yyyymmdd>.md (proposed; not yet present in repo)

status_checks_reality.finding: CI is now implemented in .github/workflows/ci.yml. It emits four deterministic, secret-free, network-free status-check contexts whose names equal the job names: `html-validate`, `catalog-schema`, `link-check`, `sw-cache-bump`. The *.yml/*.yaml files under artifacts/ remain downloadable console CONTENT and are NOT workflows that run on this repo.
status_checks_reality.note: Contexts are matched by name without integration_id, so any app reporting the context satisfies it; the CI workflow is the only producer today. develop uses a non-strict policy (rapid feedback, no forced up-to-date); preview and main are strict (branch must be up to date before merge).

_implemented_status_checks.purpose: CACHE key must change when a precached asset changes.

_proposed_status_checks.note: STILL NOT ENFORCED. External, host-generated checks. Add to a ruleset only after connecting the platform and confirming the exact generated context name.

candidates.purpose: Vercel Git integration preview/production deploy status (external check).
candidates.requires: Connect the repo in Vercel; the check context appears only once integration is enabled.

candidates.purpose: Netlify Deploy Preview status (external check; exact context is host-generated).
candidates.requires: Connect the repo in Netlify; confirm the generated context name before enforcing.

_proposed_required_deployments.note: NOT ENFORCED. Deploys currently target Vercel/Netlify/Cloudflare environments that are NOT registered as GitHub deployment environments. Once GitHub Environments named `preview` and `production` exist, a `required_deployments` rule can gate main on a successful `production` (or `preview`) deployment.

_bypass_policy.note: actor_id for actor_type RepositoryRole is the numeric role id and MUST be verified for this repo before apply. Conventionally 5=Admin, 4=Maintain, 2=Write, 1=Read. The apply script prints a reminder. On a user-owned (non-org) repo there is no OrganizationAdmin actor.
_bypass_policy.rationale: Solo-operator interim: an admin cannot approve their own PR, so a bypass is required to merge at all while there is a single maintainer. main/preview grant admin bypass ONLY in `pull_request` mode (bypass happens through a PR merge, not via direct pushes). develop grants `always` for fast integration. Tighten/remove bypass and raise main to a hard 2-reviewer gate once a second maintainer is onboarded.
